patterns.module_structure: All CLI modules are flat files in src/ (e.g., serve.rs, gantt.rs, list.rs). No cmd/ directory. Commands wired via Command enum in main.rs.
patterns.command_wiring: Each subcommand variant in Command enum routes to a function like module::cmd_foo(&root, args). See main.rs match arms.
patterns.test_pattern: #[cfg(test)] mod tests {} blocks inside each source file. Uses tempfile::tempdir() for filesystem tests.
patterns.error_handling: anyhow::Result everywhere. Use .context() for error messages. bail!() for validation errors.
patterns.http_deps: No HTTP client crate currently in project. Must add reqwest (async, matches existing tokio runtime) or ureq (blocking).
patterns.home_dir_pattern: dirs::home_dir() used in bop-core/config.rs for ~/.bop/ paths. Same pattern for ~/.claude/.
patterns.serialization: serde + serde_json throughout. #[serde(default)] and skip_serializing_if for optional fields.
patterns.existing_providers_file: providers.rs is a flat file handling .cards/providers.json (adapter selection). Must be refactored into providers/ directory module while preserving all existing code.

existing_implementations.description: providers.rs has Provider struct (adapter config), ProvidersFile, select_provider(), rotate_provider_chain(), and 16 unit tests. This is the adapter selection system, NOT the quota-monitoring system that spec 030 adds. The new Provider trait and ProviderSnapshot must coexist with the existing Provider struct (naming conflict to resolve).
existing_implementations.naming_conflict: Existing providers.rs has a struct named 'Provider' (adapter config). The spec wants a trait named 'Provider' (quota monitor). Must rename one: suggest AdapterConfig for the existing struct, or QuotaProvider for the new trait.

dependencies_to_add.reqwest: 0.12 with features [json, rustls-tls] — async HTTP client for OAuth usage API

key_decisions: Convert providers.rs to providers/mod.rs directory module (preserves all existing adapter selection code)
Rename existing Provider struct to AdapterConfig to avoid naming collision with new Provider trait
Use reqwest (not ureq) since project is already async with tokio
Provider history at ~/.bop/provider-history.jsonl (uses existing dirs::home_dir() pattern)
detect() for macOS Keychain uses security CLI subprocess (std::process::Command)
CLI command follows existing pattern: add Providers variant to Command enum, route to handler function
